question.text: Pre-emptive rights in the context of AIF investments give existing investors:

answer.text: The right to sell shares before anyone else
The right to participate proportionately in new equity issuances to maintain their ownership percentage
The right to appoint the CEO of the investee company
The right to veto all company decisions

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Pre-emptive rights allow existing investors to subscribe to new shares in proportion to their existing holding before the company offers shares to new investors. This prevents unwanted dilution. **The Trap:** Pre-emptive rights are about maintaining ownership percentage in new issuances, not about selling shares. They are a defensive mechanism, not an exit mechanism. **Cross-Exam Context:** Pre-emptive rights are tested in XIX-A and XIX-C as part of investor protection mechanisms. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Pre-emptive rights are especially important in early-stage companies that will need multiple funding rounds. AIF funds should negotiate these rights to protect investor value across future fundraising rounds.
